scenarios.scenario: Staff member manipulates request body to include a branchId and toggle availability for another branch's products
scenarios.rationale: Branch is always resolved via resolveBranchScope(db, req.staffSession!.userId) — a DB read of users.assigned_branch_id. The request body is never consulted for branch identity. Even if a client sends a branchId field, it is silently ignored. Cross-branch writes are structurally impossible.

scenarios.scenario: Staff member sends a productId belonging to an inactive product (is_active=false) to toggle availability
scenarios.rationale: PATCH /products/:productId/availability queries products WHERE id=productId AND is_active=true. An inactive product returns 404. No bpa row is created for inactive products.

scenarios.scenario: Concurrent PATCH /products/:productId/availability calls from two staff members at the same branch create duplicate bpa rows
scenarios.rationale: UPSERT uses onConflictDoUpdate targeting the uniqueIndex('bpa_branch_product_idx') on (branch_id, product_id). Postgres enforces uniqueness atomically — concurrent inserts resolve to a single row with the last writer's value. No duplicate rows possible.

scenarios.scenario: Customer role user attempts to call PATCH /api/staff/branch to disable pickup for a branch
scenarios.rationale: All /api/staff/* routes are protected by requireStaff middleware mounted in index.ts. requireStaff rejects any session where role is not in STAFF_ROLES (staff|admin|super_admin) with 403. Customer sessions are rejected before reaching any STAFF-004 handler.

scenarios.scenario: Unauthenticated request to GET /api/staff/products to enumerate product catalog
scenarios.rationale: requireStaff calls better-auth session resolution first. Unauthenticated requests have no session and are rejected with 401/403 before any handler runs.

scenarios.scenario: Staff member sets estimatedPrepMinutes to an extreme value (e.g. 0 or 9999) to manipulate ETA calculations for accepted orders
scenarios.rationale: PATCH /api/staff/branch validates estimatedPrepMinutes with z.number().int().min(1).max(120). Values outside 1–120 return 422. The accept-time ETA calculation in PATCH /orders/:orderId reads from branches.estimated_prep_minutes at accept time — any valid value in range produces a reasonable ETA.

scenarios.scenario: Staff member from branch2 calls PATCH /api/staff/branch to disable pickup for branch1 (cross-branch operational sabotage)
scenarios.rationale: Same as branchId body injection: branch is always session-derived via resolveBranchScope. Staff2's session resolves to branch2Id. The UPDATE targets branches WHERE id = branch2Id. Branch1 is never touched. Integration test AC-8 confirms isolation.
